value.text: This place was lovely! It was very busy, the music was very loud, and it was rather lively for a Sunday evening. The food was absolutely fantastic and everything was delicious. There was one hiccup where one entree (a salad) was brought to the table at the very end of the meal (after everyone else had already eaten), but the staff were intent on learning so they could fix situations like this for the next time. The wine was delicious, the focaccia was divine, and all of the entrees we had (2 gnocchi dishes and a pizza) were mouthwateringly good. I'd definitely come back, and hopefully next time, without hiccups!

value.text: I came here after hearing many recommendations. The appetizers were definitely the highlight. the octopus and hamachi were both fresh and well balanced, and the pistachio gelato at the end was truly memorable. The focaccia was a bit too crisp for my taste, almost like it had been fried, and the handmade pastas (vongole and Italian sausage) were fine but not dishes I'd come back for. Service was friendly and attentive, though I wish the dessert pricing had been clearer since the gelato was charged per scoop without being explained. I was also surprised to be handed the payment machine already set at a 25% tip; while I value good service, I prefer to decide the tip myself. Overall, it was a pleasant experience with some standouts, but there were details that left me uncertain about returning.

value.text: What a cute gem! I stopped by Yeepa & CO on a Friday after work and was so glad I did. The vibe is relaxed yet stylish—perfect for unwinding or catching up with friends. Just a heads up: there's a two-hour max seating time indoors, and the bar is first come, first serve. We chose to sit inside however, the outside seating has no time limit on patio dining (according to our server—definitely double-check when you go). The food? Delicious! We were hungry and ended up ordering two pizzas and a few appetizers—no regrets. Parking was easy, too. There's a public garage right behind the restaurant for just $5, plus some street parking and a $20 lot near the Irish pub if you're closer to that side. Great spot for a casual bite, business meet-up, or a chill night out. Be safe and enjoy!

value.text: I must say, Yeppa exceeded my expectations from the moment we arrived. We were warmly greeted by Nick, the gorgeous host or manager, who was incredibly inviting and accommodating, especially with my cousin's service dog. That welcoming attitude set the tone for our experience! Don't forget to ask for Abby best waitress ever! Very knowledgeable and helpful if you don't know what to order! Every staff member was attentive and seemed genuinely happy to be there, which added to the overall positive atmosphere. Now, let's talk about the food. At first glance, the pizza may look simple, but one bite reveals a delightful surprise! The homemade crust is airy and fluffy, perfectly seasoned, and the combination of homemade mozzarella l, fresh basil and that fresh homemade sauce is indescribable – it just makes you want to keep eating it! Next up were the steak skewers, which were nothing short of divine. They were incredibly tender, and that chimichurri sauce! I can drink it! You'll want a bottle to take home! I also tried the homemade pasta with clams, and oh my God, it was cooked to perfection. This place truly exemplifies Italian cuisine at its best. One unexpected highlight was the roasted cauliflower, which we hadn't planned to order but ended up being the star of the show. I'm definitely returning just for that! Oh, and if you go on a Tuesday, don't miss out on their half-off bottles of wine! We had a bottle of Pinot Noir, and it was a fantastic choice. I'm already planning my return; Yeppa will definitely be our new meeting spot, ladies!

value.text: The ambiance at Yeppa and Co. is very lively and robust. The restaurant is dark, on the inside, yet still bright due to the multiple red LED lights and table lights. This makes for it being a great environment for a date or a night out with friends. The drinks were okay at best, I got the Sangria and my friend got the espresso martini which she enjoyed. The rosemary foccacia is light, crispy, and flaky. The sea salt is well paired in this which makes it a light appetizer. The caprese salad was light, but a bit too vinegary. The ricotta gnocchi was creamy and light, not super doughy like your usual gnocchi— I think the sauce could use a bit of pepper. Overall, this is a great, fun place to grab dinner or drinks. It is pretty loud, and it gets very busy at night so make sure to get reservations for dinner.

value.text: Yeppa & Co. in Buckhead was a wonderful experience overall. The chic, hip vibe fits the area perfectly, the portions were just right, and the pasta dishes — especially the pesto and the limone — were absolutely phenomenal. Everything came out hot and fresh. Our waitress, Shelby, was fantastic — helpful, knowledgeable about the menu, and kind. For dessert, the fig cheesecake wasn't my favorite (too gelatinous and lacking fig flavor), but the gelatos were exceptional — smooth, flavorful, and melt-in-your-mouth good. Honestly, they should sell them to take home! The only downside was that the music was a little too loud, which made conversations difficult. Overall, Yeppa & Co. is a true delight and definitely worth a visit.

They complimented each other very well - flavorful but not overpowering (portion sizes were great,too) I went to the buckhead location, but there's a midtown location opening soon on the beltline. Great for a solo date on the town and even to bring out the family.

value.text: I stopped into Yeppa the other day, just for a beer - and it was so well worth it, I even stretched to two. Firstly, I can't speak on the food, because I did not, but I have been to Pietro's other restaurants and I the food is always on par w/ the best. And, I wouldn't expect here to be any different. As for the beer and the bar. One of the many things I love most here are the aesthetic throughout the restaurant. The atmosphere, the environment, and the colors all complement and flow in a way that says, “hey, why don't you stick around and order more stuff?” Justin (bartender) was very attentive, welcoming, knowledgeable, and conversation-able. The beer itself was not one I had heard over or tried before, it was smooth, and presented elegantly w/ 65-70% into a neat glass, coupled w/ a water. Spectacular. Beautiful. Top-notch.

value.text: If you are looking for a quiet and intimate place for dinner, this is not it. However, if you are looking for a vibrant atmosphere with excellent food and a party vibe, this most definitely is the place! value.text: Yeppa & Co. is one of those places that reminds you why Buckhead's cocktail scene is worth paying attention to. I've been a few times now, and it's consistently solid. The vibe is exactly what you want for a more mature evening out—low lighting, comfortable seating, and a real focus on the craft of the drinks rather than just being a place to be seen. The bartenders clearly know their stuff; they're efficient and can guide you through the menu if you're unsure, which is helpful with their creative list. I appreciated that the cocktails are inventive without being overly complicated, letting the quality of the spirits shine through. It's the kind of spot you'd take someone for a proper conversation or to close a deal, not to get rowdy. While it's certainly not a cheap night, the experience and quality make it a justified choice for a special occasion or when you just want a perfectly made drink.

value.text: Enjoyed the outside seating for an early Saturday dinner around 4:30 and the place was packed out front with lots of seating inside. We saw some parties waiting to be seated and they were probably not able to sit them in the empty areas due to staffing. We had a great service from our friendly and informative waitress. Also great interactions with other waiters. The patrons appeared happy and very lively area on the front porch. Lots of dog owners set with their fur babies at the tables out on the walkway. The food was excellent. From the charcuterie board to the pasta dishes, non were a disappointment. Fried polenta is a must get item with a soft inner and crunchy outer. Our charcuterie selection has this sweet ricotta dolce spread that was like butter and tastes amazing with the focaccia bread, meats and cheeses. The skewers were good but a bit over priced, still worth a try. Even though I loved my meaty ragu with chewy strozzapreti noodles, I would have to say Elly's linguini alle vongole was the winner among the two. It had a nice hint of white wine and some hit of spiciness, and the small little clams were excellent. One down side is if you are a soda drinker, be ready to fork up $3 for a tiny 7.5 oz can. I should have bought a cocktail instead. I wouldn't have minded a normal can 12oz can with no refills. Everything else was priced as expected for the quality of food and location. Definite good reasons why this is so highly rated on Google. Parking is validated for 2 hours and you within the same bldg structure with 2 sides. The deck behind the restaurant is under ground, so don't get confused and try to enter the first ramp up for residents only, go further in the alley way and turn right to go underground.

value.text: I went to Yeppa on a Tuesday night and was pleasantly surprised by the lively atmosphere. The restaurant had a live DJ, which added to the ambiance, but at times, it made it challenging to have a conversation. However, the food and service more than made up for it. The pizza was a definite standout; the crispy dough, flavorful sauce, and high-quality cheese made it a must-try. Additionally, the seafood entree was a hit, with the sauce garnering its own accolades. Personally, I ordered the linguine with clams, and while the sauce was fantastic, I felt that the clams could have been fresher. I had to chew them a little more than I would have liked, which did take away some points from the dish. It was a small letdown in an otherwise delightful dining experience. The service was top-notch though, adding to the overall fantastic dining experience. I would highly recommend Yeppa & Co for anyone looking for great Italian food in a vibrant setting.

value.text: Hands down a wonderful experience from beginning to end . The hostesses were sweet and welcoming our waiter Jimmy (I believe was his name ) was helpful, informative & gave us excellent service. We opted for the three tier Wednesday pasta special the freshness of the pasta was unmatched and I've been to restaurants in Atlanta that makes fresh pasta in house . Star of the show definitely the tortellini but they we're all oh so tasty! For appetizer we got The small margarita pizza which was delicious the crust was so soft almost like focaccia bread, and flavorful. The calamari on a bed of fried onions was the perfect amount of salt , crisp and flavor . For drinks i opted for a sweetwater IPA . My sister got the frozé which she enjoyed. For dessert we each ordered 'agelato' unfortunately except for the chocolate the vanilla tasted like flavorless milk water with hints of chocolate due to the topping and the strawberry tasted like very sweet freeze dried strawberry milk water . Sadly a huge miss in the dessert department. But either way the ambiance and vibe of the restaurant was phenomenal definitely worth every penny and not so great gelato.

We opted for the three tier Wednesday pasta special the freshness of the pasta was unmatched and I've been to restaurants in Atlanta that makes fresh pasta in house . Star of the show definitely the tortellini but they we're all oh so tasty! For appetizer we got The small margarita pizza which was delicious the crust was so soft almost like focaccia bread, and flavorful. The calamari on a bed of fried onions was the perfect amount of salt , crisp and flavor . For drinks i opted for a sweetwater IPA . My sister got the frozé which she enjoyed. For dessert we each ordered 'agelato' unfortunately except for the chocolate the vanilla tasted like flavorless milk water with hints of chocolate due to the topping and the strawberry tasted like very sweet freeze dried strawberry milk water . Sadly a huge miss in the dessert department. But either way the ambiance and vibe of the restaurant was phenomenal definitely worth every penny and not so great gelato.

value.text: I really enjoyed every part of my meal at Yeppa & Co. I reserved an outdoor table which turned out to be absolutely perfect on a gorgeous Friday night. Yeppa was quite crowded and a little loud but being on the patio meant a bit of fresh air and people watching. The frose was some of my favorite I've had with its extra splash of lemon flavor. The polenta fries were unique. I loved the Margherita on focaccia. The pizza 'crust' was a lovely fluffy texture with little bits of crunch on the outsides. The Cacio e Pepe was also very good but it could have used a little more pepper to enhance the flavor. I think my review is biased because of the outdoor setting on a pretty day but the food was also very memorable.

value.text: Our visit began with anticipation, as the pleasant weather set a perfect backdrop for our outing. The restaurant's ambiance was warm and inviting, contributing to our excitement. Josue's attentiveness ensured that we felt at ease right from the start, and his friendly manner quickly established a rapport. Our initial conversation was intriguing. He mentioned Macon and Italy together, which seemed impossible. His stories about life, creativity, and philosophy enriched our appreciation of the place. The culinary delights surpassed our expectations, with each dish meticulously prepared and bursting with flavor. We savored the meal, appreciating the chef's expertise in crafting such exquisite dishes. Additionally, Henry's music added an unexpected yet delightful layer to our dining experience. His talent and choice of tunes perfectly complemented the setting, creating a harmonious atmosphere. Overall, our visit was memorable not only because of the exceptional food but also due to the genuine connections we made with the staff. It was clear that this restaurant values its patrons and strives to provide an all-encompassing enjoyment beyond just the meal. We left with full hearts and satisfied palates, eager to return and relive the wonderful moments.

value.text: I recently visited this spot and one staff member truly stood out. Upon arrival, the hostess wasn't very flexible in helping us secure a table inside. However, Jonathan, our server, went above and beyond to make our experience much more enjoyable. He noticed we were uncomfortable with the cold and immediately brought over a heater to keep us warm. Going even further, Jonathan offered us his own coat to ensure we were comfortable throughout our meal. His kindness and attention to detail truly made the difference, and I can honestly say he turned our experience around. Jonathan's warmth and generosity were more than enough to leave us with a positive impression of the restaurant. Food was great too. Highly recommend!

value.text: I cannot say enough great things about the Yeppa Buckhead location. From the moment we walked in, we were warmly welcomed, immediately seated, and served with true hospitality—a complete 180 from the disappointing experience we had at the Beltline location. Nickolas Boobas, the General Manager, truly sets the tone for excellence—his professionalism and care were obvious from the start. Danny also went above and beyond to ensure we felt valued and comfortable. Their attention to detail, speed, and genuine kindness turned our night around completely. I will never not go to the Buckhead Yeppa—this location has earned a loyal customer for life. The Beltline was a waste of time, but Buckhead reminded us what a great night out should feel like. Thank you again to the amazing team for showing how it's done right!

value.text: This is definitely one of the TOP restaurants in the country! I am only waiting for it to get a Michelin star. The complexity of even the simplest of items is unreal. value.text: My family and I did an impromptu visit to Yeppa and boy am I glad we did. At first we had a bit of trouble getting a table as it was quite busy due to the French market, however, the manager Miranna worked hard to secure us a spot. I also observed her take care of other families with small little ones which I must say was admirable as it's often hard to find a place that accommodates children with a smile. Ok now on to the food. Mussels 10/10, calamari 10/10, non alcoholic spritz 8/10 and my son had a margarita pizza that he said was really good. We will be back again with the entire family. Thank you for a delicious lunch and great hospitality.

So, I enjoy the food at Yeppa. Based off food alone, this is a great spot. But Yeppa management & some staff are racist. My friend and I, both black women, were trying to sit at the bar but the manager told us 'the bar is closed.' We walked away. We saw two white men ask the same manager if the bar was open and he said 'Absolutely! Come in!'. The hostess was rude as well. I wonder if the manager is teaching the staff to avoid the business of black women. Just a thought.

value.text: rushing us out of the table

data.text: Food quality and service have drastically declined while the prices have only gone up. This place is overhyped and overdone. It'started with a rude interaction with hostess/door staff slamming their arm in front of me aggressively telling me the bar was full and we couldn't go inside when I was just trying to check in for our reservation. I should have listened to my intuition then and just left because it only got worse from there. The pizzas are now one size, more expensive, and lacking toppings/cheese, even more than they already were. Drinks are small and weak and they had a limited selection of liquors - 2 things my husband ordered ended up being out of stock so it took 40 minutes to even get our first drink, we went with a bottle of wine for the next round so we didn't have to keep waiting. Our food took over an hour and when we spoke to a manger he gave us a snarky, fake apology and said it's Saturday, everywhere is busy. I'm sorry but a hour is plenty of time to prepare a Cacio E Pepe pasta dish and Margarita Pizza, especially charging the prices they are for average quality food. Once we finally got our food, they were rushing us out of the table before we could even finish our bottle of wine. Most disappointing restaurant experience I've had in a while and the bad vibes ruined our date night out. I understand the need for clearing tables for other reservations, but when you don't even get your food til 45 mins before the allotment is up, they should be more understanding. 0/5 stars for customer service, 1/5 stars for food & drinks because they were at least edible.

value.text: was not even properly set up

data.text: We made reservations, but still had to wait over 15 minutes for our table which was not even properly set up. Everyone's dinner arrived at different times which made it awkward. The food was mediocre at best, nothing special. The waitress was polite but extremely slow and detached. The place was extremely loud so conversation across the table was difficult. We chose this restaurant because of its very high ratings by over thousands of reviews, but I cannot figure out why it received any stars.

value.text: She really should be working at Cracker Barrel.

data.text: Maria is by far the worst waitress/server in the region. She really should be working at Cracker Barrel. We have been coming to yeppa for a long time. We usually sit with Danny who is truly an asset to the company. Maria spilled our martini and didn't apologize only stating ' I will bring another one', we said thankyou a couple times only to notice she doesn't respond with a 'your welcome,' or 'ofcourse'. She was very rude , so rude we intentionally said thankyou for her bringing a new drink after spilling it , & took a recording just to prove how rude she is. Thankyou to Nick who handled everything with professionalism and kindness, maybe Maria needs more training or perhaps a job at McDonalds, because I'm sure chic fil a wouldn't even hire her, simply because she's probably incapable of saying 'my pleasure.'

value.text: There wasn't a degree of professionalism in the way he spoke.

data.text: Been coming here for a while, but after what I saw over the weekend, I wont be back. Watching a manager aggressively talk down to two women over a bar seat one had clearly been waiting on was disrespectful and completely out of line. There wasn't a degree of professionalism in the way he spoke. I regret not saying something in the moment but it made me just as uncomfortable. If that's how they handle customers, it's no surprise why the staff is always turning over. Plenty of better bars in this city. Food is mediocre at best. Do better Yeppa & Co.

After many great experiences at Yeppa, I had a very disappointing one this evening. Yeppa is quite popular, and since we often don't make reservations, we usually sit at the bar. When I approached the hostess to express our interest in sitting at the bar, she informed me that they were at capacity. However, she made no effort to check for available seating. After deciding to go to another restaurant, we waited outside for our Uber and watched as they escorted other guests to the bar. While we were waiting, two other Black patrons approached the hostess but were also turned away. Whether this is a matter of racial bias or simply poor customer service, I am incredibly disappointed in the owners and staff.

data.text: Refried Branzino! This was my first time eating Branzino and even I can tell that it was not fresh. It reeked of grease like a fast food burger. Yet somehow, it was raw in the center. What a magnificent feat. It was absolutely curated wrongly. The fennel salad on top was salty. The olives, salty and unnecessary for the dish. The side of mashed potatoes with garlic that I ordered looked thawed from frozen. I had the waiter just take them away. The green beans had some sort of odd taste and I had an entire new plate done and to my dismay overly salted. Based on my food today I would never return and run from here every time I saw their sign.

Pasta portions are small and overpriced- I ordered the Strozzapreti Con Ragù Di Salsiccia it was cold when it arrived and the pasta seemed under cooked and didn't taste the same as before. Oh it's also super loud & the space is small we were sat right beside the bar -next to the entrance where tables are placed super close to each other & ppl are walking by every second.

The drinks are very small, food was cold and lacked flavor. I had cacio e pepe which was cold, small portion and lacked flavor, and a Negroni which was okay but only halfway full. Servers were pretty nice but had this weird incident where our server made and extra cocktail and asked if we 'want' it we said yes and she charged it…weird since she made the mistake of making an extra one. Over all I will never visit again and I don't recommend it.
Their gluten free options are not truly gluten free. I went with the recommendation of the waiter and have been sick for a few days since.

Food and drinks were delicious however - after ordering several drinks, several appetizers, main courses and dessert, we had someone come to our table not 2 minutes after paying the bill ousting us out saying he really needed the table. Totally ruined our experience. We had just spent hundreds as a table and we were literally pushed out. I'll stick to Storico Fresco from now on and you should too!

value.text: we had to wait over an hour for our food

data.text: Fun atmosphere and awesome drinks! Our server was kind but we had to wait over an hour for our food. We ordered eggs and potatoes and an omelette. Really unfortunate experience, I feel like maybe we just slipped through the cracks. Looked like other patrons were having fun. The manager did comp our meal which was kind. Will not be returning as the food was not good once it finally arrived.

This was one of my favorite spots to grab lunch since I live so close and the food is actually very good. The cocktails are excellent too. However, yesterday for lunch I went with a coworker and she asked a hostess to check on a drink we had ordered and was taking a while to come out. The hostess shot back that she wasn't our server and rolled her eyes at us. Totally understand not being able to assist us directly and grabbing the server but no need to be rude about it. This place is pricey so for the meantime I'll take my business elsewhere. Plenty of alternatives in this space like Gypsy kitchen or le colonial where I've never had an experience like this.

This establishment SUCKS!!!!!!!! They allowed dogs to sit at the table while eating your food. This is not mentioned on their website. Neither did the hostess share this information to my party. The food is HORRIBLE!!!!!! Then, I had a huge black and white dog from a table beside me came over and put his nose in my food. I LOST MY MIND!!!!!! Thereafter, I shared this with the manger and he just said, “ Oh yes we do allow dog!” I had to walk out before I caught a case. Trying something new was totally RUINED!!!
If you like mediocre, overpriced small portion Italian food and being around basic white girls taking instagram pics this is a great spot for you. Otherwise, pass

We had a great server. But our pasta came out really quickly. And as I was eating it, it was hot on the top and then cold towards the center. My friend had the same experience w her pasta. I don't think that their pasta is made to order. It's reheated. Love taverna! And even their is sister restaurant storico fresco is better and They have some of the same menu options for less. Paying $30 for reheated pasta Was disappointing. That's the only way you can explain it being so hot and the minute I stick my fork in it it was cold in the center

value.text: it's like not having a ramp to accommodate a wheelchair

data.text: It was so heartbreaking for us when our daughter called yesterday and asked us to fetch her early from a bday party at Yeppa. She said the restaurant was lovely and the food was good but it was so loud and she just could not hear anything and it was impossible to feel part of the group. Our daughter is 15 years old and lost her hearing 2 years ago. She wears a cochlear implant. When the music is very loud it makes it impossible to hear. Most times she just needs it turned down a little bit to help her a whole lot. Most restaurants are so kind and accommodating. She didn't ask that the music be turned off, just that it be lowered a little on her side for the hour they were eating. They just said plain 'no, we don't do that'. In my mind, it's like not having a ramp to accommodate a wheelchair. It's already tough for a hearing impaired teen. It took great courage at her age to ask and it was disappointing that this was the response. It's not only about food, it's also about creating an environment that's inclusive for all to enjoy. The response of your waitress was just unkind and insensitive and the next time this 15 year old needs help, she will be less likely to ask. disappointing

value.text: It's expensive for the quality of the food.

Went on a Saturday around brunch time, tried ordering 2 different appetizers (the frito mixto & veal balls) told me they ran out of these. Ordered pasta on 2 different occasions, not very tasty. The salsa lacks flavor. Unfortunately, this time the waiter wasn't great either which was the cherry on top. Asked for olive oil twice for bread service, didn't get it until after we were done with the bread. Asked for tomato sauce, never received it. Had to ask another waiter for water. Vibes are nice for a drink. But not for food, won't be going back. Not worth it.

We all ordered different pasta plates and our noodles were tough . Very much a hype restaurant for white ppl. The servers were doing their best and were kind but the crowd wasn't pleasant. Very much fratty.

value.text: stay away from taking a new date here

data.text: I'm young and I still thought it was way too LOUD. They had a live DJ blasting music like it was dance club. If eating at a dance club is your scene, then you'll like it. value.text: can't get drinks out to its tables in a timely manner

data.text: Had a reservation at 8pm arrived 5 minutes early and did not get seated until 10 minutes after 8pm- waiting outside in the cold that whole time. Once inside it was very loud, crowded, and dark. The music was so loud we could barely have a conversation at our table. Our order was taken for drinks and apps, we received our app right away. We finished our app and still did not have our drinks. We waited over 20 minutes with no one updating us on if our drinks were coming or not. After waiting almost 25 minutes with no drinks or service our waiter came and said our drinks were on the way. We asked for our check due to not knowing if we were ever going to get our drinks. Wouldn't recommend eating here if you want drinks with dinner. Yeppa seems to have an overcrowded bar that can't get drinks out to its tables in a timely manner.

Bring back the Vegetable Panini or I will not come back. It was by far the best dish. Now there are almost no vegetarian options anymore. The servers keep saying that people ask where it went all the time. Bring it back please.

value.text: For the price point I expect more

data.text: Spent $200+ on lunch with bottle of wine and still left hungry. Portion sizes are very small, food is just okay. Not terrible and not amazing. For the price point I expect more. Cross use of ingredients in various entrees and appetizers makes everything seem the same. Literally had 3 things from different parts of the menu that has the same exact pesto sauce! Tagliatelle pasta with Ragu sauce was just okay. Pasta was overcooked and it was literally swimming in oil. It took me 5 bar napkins and tilting my bowl to soak up all the oil. Beef skewer was good, fennel salad was just okay. We opted for wine because if you saw the size of the teeny tiny cocktails, you would do the same. You'll go broke trying to get a buzz in this place. Bottom line, there are way better places to eat in buckhead where you get your moneys worth! This place is all hyped up right now because it's the new thing on the block, but I doubt they will survive.

We ended getting done with dinner nearby much earlier than I expected so we went over there to see if we could get in earlier. We were told we had to wait until our reservation regardless and the hostess was rude about it. My husband loves formula one so I had been trying to surprised him which was ruined by this. However, considering they don't show the formula 1 races, I don't know how legitimate this bar really is for true formula 1 fans.
